WHERE sc.NumTstTakr <> 0

LIMIT 3;

FROM schools s
JOIN satscores sc ON s.CDSCode = sc.cds

samples.prediction:
SELECT
    s.CDSCode,
    s.CharterNum
FROM
    schools s
JOIN
    satscores t ON s.CDSCode = t.cds
WHERE
    t.AvgScrWrite > 499
ORDER BY
    t.AvgScrWrite DESC;
samples.expected: SELECT CharterNum, AvgScrWrite, RANK() OVER (ORDER BY AvgScrWrite DESC) AS WritingScoreRank FROM schools AS T1  INNER JOIN satscores AS T2 ON T1.CDSCode = T2.cds WHERE T2.AvgScrWrite > 499 AND CharterNum is not null
samples.raw_output:
SELECT
    s.CDSCode,
    s.CharterNum
FROM
    schools s
JOIN
    satscores t ON s.CDSCode = t.cds
WHERE
    t.AvgScrWrite > 499
ORDER BY
    t.AvgScrWrite DESC;

samples.prediction:
SELECT (COUNT(*) * 100.0) / (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM set_translations WHERE language = 'Japanese') AS percentage
FROM set_translations st
JOIN sets s ON st.setCode = s.code
WHERE st.language = 'Japanese' AND s.type = 'expansion';
samples.expected: SELECT CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN T2.language = 'Japanese' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) * 100 / COUNT(T1.id) FROM sets AS T1 INNER JOIN set_translations AS T2 ON T1.code = T2.setCode WHERE T1.type = 'expansion'
samples.raw_output:
SELECT (COUNT(*) * 100.0) / (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM set_translations WHERE language = 'Japanese') AS percentage
FROM set_translations st
JOIN sets s ON st.setCode = s.code
WHERE st.language = 'Japanese' AND s.type = 'expansion';

samples.prediction:
SELECT
    SUBSTR(t.Date,5,2) AS month,
    MAX(t.Consumption) AS peak_consumption
FROM
    yearmonth t
JOIN
    customers c
ON
    t.CustomerID = c.CustomerID
WHERE
    c.Segment = 'SME'
    AND SUBSTR(t.Date,1,4) = '2013'
GROUP BY
    month
ORDER BY
    peak_consumption DESC
LIMIT 1;
samples.expected: SELECT SUBSTR(T2.Date, 5, 2) FROM customers AS T1 INNER JOIN yearmonth AS T2 ON T1.CustomerID = T2.CustomerID WHERE SUBSTR(T2.Date, 1, 4) = '2013' AND T1.Segment = 'SME' GROUP BY SUBSTR(T2.Date, 5, 2) ORDER BY SUM(T2.Consumption) DESC LIMIT 1
samples.raw_output:
SELECT
    SUBSTR(t.Date,5,2) AS month,
    MAX(t.Consumption) AS peak_consumption
FROM
    yearmonth t
JOIN
    customers c
ON
    t.CustomerID = c.CustomerID
WHERE
    c.Segment = 'SME'
    AND SUBSTR(t.Date,1,4) = '2013'
GROUP BY
    month
ORDER BY
    peak_consumption DESC
LIMIT 1;

samples.prediction:
SELECT COUNT(*) 
FROM budget b
JOIN event e ON b.link_to_event = e.event_id
WHERE b.category = 'Advertisement'
  AND e.event_name IN ('Yearly Kickoff', 'October Meeting')
  AND b.amount > (
    (SELECT SUM(b.amount) 
     FROM budget b
     JOIN event e ON b.link_to_event = e.event_id
     WHERE e.event_name = 'Yearly Kickoff')
    -
    (SELECT SUM(b.amount) 
     FROM budget b
     JOIN event e ON b.link_to_event = e.event_id
     WHERE e.event_name = 'October Meeting')
  );
samples.expected: SELECT CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN T2.event_name = 'Yearly Kickoff' THEN T1.amount ELSE 0 END) AS REAL) / SUM(CASE WHEN T2.event_name = 'October Meeting' THEN T1.amount ELSE 0 END) FROM budget AS T1 INNER JOIN event AS T2 ON T1.link_to_event = T2.event_id WHERE T1.category = 'Advertisement' AND T2.type = 'Meeting'
samples.raw_output:
SELECT COUNT(*) 
FROM budget b
JOIN event e ON b.link_to_event = e.event_id
WHERE b.category = 'Advertisement'
  AND e.event_name IN ('Yearly Kickoff', 'October Meeting')
  AND b.amount > (
    (SELECT SUM(b.amount) 
     FROM budget b
     JOIN event e ON b.link_to_event = e.event_id
     WHERE e.event_name = 'Yearly Kickoff')
    -
    (SELECT SUM(b.amount) 
     FROM budget b
     JOIN event e ON b.link_to_event = e.event_id
     WHERE e.event_name = 'October Meeting')
  );
